title: WORD-DRIFT — An Open Knowledge Graph for Lexical Semantic Change
description: WORD-DRIFT is an open RDF/SHACL knowledge graph for lexical semantic change (Bedeutungswandel) in German and English. It models individual word senses with diachronic attestation intervals and connotation values, typed drift events (pejoration, amelioration, broadening, narrowing, metaphorization, metonymization, reversal, reappropriation), and — as its novel contribution — a causal trigger layer linking real-world events to the drift they plausibly caused. Each causal claim is a confidence-graded hypothesis with a source citation, never an asserted fact (ADR-0004). Built on OntoLex-Lemon, OWL-Time, PROV-O and SKOS.
notes: PREPARED metadata only — not yet deposited. Upstream source datasets (DWUG, SemEval, DWDS, Google Books Ngrams, OWID, Wikidata) retain their own licenses; WORD-DRIFT redistributes only derived, transformed triples. Counts are generated from data/reports/stats.json via `make metadata`.
